You are running as a single automated phase of the ADW pipeline.
The orchestrator performs ALL git and GitHub work for this run: do NOT run git or gh, do NOT create/switch/commit/push branches, and do NOT open, merge, or comment on pull requests. If the task section below tells you to do any of that, skip those steps.
You have no GitHub access in this phase; all issue context you need is provided inline.

---

Classify work item #4 into a single change type for branch naming and commit prefixing.

Work item context (title, body, labels):

4 GitHub issue #4: test: failure-drill scaffold (timeout/budget/resume)
Labels: issue_class:test adw-live-batch

Part of the MVP live-run batch (`adw_sdlc/docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md`). Intended `issue_class`: **test**. Planned ADW run mode: native.

## Goal
Provide a small, documented procedure (and any helper test) to exercise ADW failure modes live: `--timeout` fast-fail, `--max-budget-usd` cap, and kill-then-`--resume`.

## Scope
- New `adw_sdlc/docs/FAILURE-DRILLS.md` describing each drill, the exact command, and the expected signal (`timeout` / `budget` / resume skips completed phases).
- Optional: a focused vitest that asserts the invoker maps `signal:'timeout'` / `signal:'budget'` to fail-fast with no nudge (if not already covered) — extend `test/run-phase.test.ts` rather than duplicate.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each of the three drills has a copy-paste command and expected outcome.
- [ ] Any added test is deterministic (mocked runner; no network/spend).
- [ ] `npm run verify` stays green.

## Notes
Do NOT instruct the agent to violate the output contract to force a nudge-retry; that drill is opportunistic.

## Instructions

- Choose exactly one `issue_class` from: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `test`, `refactor`.
  - `feat` — new user-visible feature or capability.
  - `fix` — bug fix or correctness change.
  - `docs` — documentation-only change.
  - `chore` — maintenance, deps, tooling with no user-visible behavior change.
  - `ci` — CI/build/workflow change.
  - `test` — test-only change.
  - `refactor` — internal restructuring with no behavior change.
- Prefer the narrowest truthful classification.
- If labels and body disagree, classify by the actual requested change.
